name: Front-end Dev with React & GenAI Advanced Program

issues.Content: Master Front-End Web Development with React, Responsive Design, Modern JavaScript, and Generative AI Integration.
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'Build entry-level Java & Front-end Developer skills across SQL, Core and Object-Oriented Java, Data Structures and Algorithms, modern JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3, and React — designed to make you job-ready.'

issues.Content: Not present on page
issues.Gap / Issue: The product description's program sub-headline 'Outcome Driven; Practitioner Designed' is not surfaced on the page.
issues.Fix Suggested: Add: 'Outcome Driven; Practitioner Designed' as a sub-headline below the program name.

issues.Content: The Front-End Development with React & GenAI Advanced Program by NIIT is a future-ready program with placement assistance that equips learners with in-demand skills to design, develop, and deploy responsive, interactive web applications using modern JavaScript, React, and Generative AI tools. Through hands-on projects and industry-aligned training, participants gain practical experience and job-ready expertise for careers in web and AI-driven application development.
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'This program builds core competencies for entry-level Java and Front-end Developer roles, covering SQL, Java, HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and React. Learners develop strong technical foundations alongside essential engineering and professional skills. The curriculum progresses from databases and object-oriented programming to data structures, advanced Java, responsive web development, and React-based applications.'

issues.Gap / Issue: Count is less

issues.Content: Page curriculum lists only 3 courses (Web Dev, React SPAs, Navigable React).
issues.Gap / Issue: The description defines a 7-course program. The first four courses — Managing and Querying Database (RDBMS), Java: Object-Oriented Programming, Data Structures and Algorithms, Apply Advanced Java Concepts – Streams and Lambdas — are entirely absent from the page curriculum, even though the description names the program 'Java & Front-end Developer'.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Add four course entries before the existing three:
1. Managing and Querying Database (RDBMS) — Weeks 1–4 — 60 Hours
2. Java: Object-Oriented Programming — Weeks 4–8 — 60 Hours
3. Data Structures and Algorithms — Weeks 8–11 — 52 Hours
4. Apply Advanced Java Concepts – Streams and Lambdas — Weeks 11–14 — 40 Hours
Each with 2–3 bullets following the standard format (sub-heading on its own line, 1–2 sentence prose), an Activity, and a course-end Project card.
issues.Remarks: The content for all 4 courses (MySQL, OOPs, Data Structure and Advance java needs to be taken from - Java Development Certificate Program

issues.Content: Web Development and UI/UX Essentials
issues.Fix Suggested: Rename to: 'Building Responsive and Interactive Web Pages'

issues.Content: Weeks 1–4 — 60 hours
issues.Fix Suggested: Update to: 'Course 5: Weeks 14–17 — 60 Hours' once the four Java courses are added.

issues.Content: • Practical skills to build responsive web pages — Learn to structure, style, and prototype responsive web interfaces using HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, Bootstrap, and UI/UX design principles with Figma.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with:
• Responsive Web Design & User Experience
Create visually appealing and responsive web interfaces by structuring content effectively and applying modern styling practices aligned with UI/UX principles.
• Interactive Web Development & API Integration
Build dynamic, interactive web applications by handling user interactions and seamlessly connecting front-end interfaces with backend services through APIs.

issues.Content: Activity — Build a fully responsive, multi-page website applying UI/UX principles, wireframes, layouts, and modern frontend styling techniques.
issues.Gap / Issue: Activity label is correct ('Activity', not 'Module Activity'). No fix needed for label, but the activity description is generic.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with: 'Activity
Hands-on Project to Choose: BookBack – Local Book Exchange Website, Cookify – Recipe Sharing Website, SkillSetu – Local Skills for Hire Website, TableTap – Online table-booking site'

issues.Content: Build Single-Page Applications Using React
issues.Fix Suggested: Rename to: 'Building Single Page Applications Using React'

issues.Content: Weeks 5–7 — 45 hours
issues.Fix Suggested: Update to: 'Course 6: Weeks 18–21 — 60 Hours'

issues.Content:
Activity
Develop a dynamic SPA with reusable React components, state-driven UI updates, and persistent application data handling.
issues.Gap / Issue: Should list project titles
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with: 'Activity 
Hands-on Project to Choose: Bake My Cake – Digital catalog for cakes, cookies, and brownies, BookBuddy – Campus bookstore web app, DailyDash Lite – Habit & mood heatmap tracker, Dairy Delights – Organic dairy product catalog'

issues.Content: • Develop dynamic applications using React fundamentals — Create interactive Single Page Applications using React concepts such as components, props, state management, hooks, and declarative rendering.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with below:
• Component-Based UI Development
Build reusable and maintainable user interfaces using JSX, components, and props.

• State, Interactivity & Data Handling
Create dynamic apps by managing state, handling interactions, and integrating APIs with React hooks.

• Scalable, Styled & Tested Apps
Improve reliability with TypeScript, design responsive UIs using Material UI, and ensure quality through testing using Jest and RTL.

issues.Content: Building SPA with Navigable Workflows Using React
issues.Fix Suggested: Rename to:"Building SPA with Complex Forms and Navigable Workflows Using React"

issues.Content: Weeks 8–9 — 30 hours
issues.Fix Suggested: Update to: 'Course 7: Weeks 21–24 — 40 Hours'

issues.Content: • Implement scalable navigation and advanced React workflows — Design structured applications using React Router, form handling, component styling with CSS Modules, and user interaction workflows.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with below content:
• Forms & Navigation in React Applications
Build and validate complex forms and enable seamless user journeys through efficient form handling and in-app routing.

• Authentication, State Management & Performance
Implement secure access control and manage application state effectively using Context API while optimizing performance for production-ready apps.

issues.Content:
Activity
Build a workflow-based React application with multi-page navigation, validated forms, and seamless user experience flows.
issues.Gap / Issue: Should list project titles
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with: 'Activity 
Hands-on Project to Choose: EventRadar — Campus Events Discovery, HostelBites Lite — Smart Mess Manager, MovieMate Lite — Personal Movie Watchlist, SpendSense Viewer — Expense Insights  Dashboard, TaskTrackr — Smart To-Do Dashboard'

issues.Content: Explore industry-relevant tools through hands-on learning to master practical, in-demand skills.
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'Explore industry-relevant tools through hands-on learning to build practical, in-demand skills.'

issues.Content: Listed on page: HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, React, OpenAI.
issues.Fix Suggested: Add: 'MySQL, Maven, JUnit, Bootstrap, Figma, Vite, TypeScript, Axios, Material UI, Jest, React Testing Library,  React Hook Form, GenAI Tools'

issues.Content: Capstone Project — Leverage Generative AI tools to generate React components, debug JavaScript code, create UI documentation, optimize performance, and accelerate development of responsive web applications.
issues.Gap / Issue: Program has no Capstone Project course in the product description — the description shows 7 courses, each with its own Course-End Project.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Add six project cards:
1. Fitness Tracker Database - Design a fitness tracker database managing workouts, health data, subscriptions, and personalized progress tracking insights.
2.  Cookify – Recipe Sharing Web Platform - Design a responsive recipe-sharing web platform with browsing, contribution, and async data handling features.
3. EcoRide – Create a Java ride-sharing simulator with role-based menus for drivers and riders including booking and validation.
4. Online Polling System with Real-Time Analytics – Build a real-time polling system with vote processing, duplicate prevention, instant analytics, and rollback capabilities.
5. MovieMate Lite — Personal Movie Watchlist – Build a responsive React app using components, state, and hooks to browse, filter, and manage a personalized movie watchlist.
6. FarmConnect – Design a farmer-to-buyer marketplace for listing, ordering, and tracking fresh produce with inventory management.

issues.Content: 3+ Projects
issues.Gap / Issue: No. of projects offered are more
issues.Fix Suggested: Update to: '20+ Projects' once all 7 courses are surfaced

issues.Content: Boost Your Career Visibility — Showcasing a professional Capstone Project on your LinkedIn increases recruiter interest significantly. Build production-ready work that speaks for itself.
issues.Gap / Issue: Callout uses the term 'Capstone Project' which does not exist as a course in this program.
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'Boost Your Career Visibility — Showcasing your professional Projects on your LinkedIn increases recruiter interest significantly. Build production-ready work that speaks for itself.'

issues.Content: Page outcomes: 1) Design and develop responsive, user-friendly web interfaces…  2) Build dynamic SPAs using React… 3) Implement navigable workflows… 4) Apply UI/UX principles… 5) Utilize Generative AI tools to accelerate coding, debugging, testing, and technical documentation. 6) Develop and deploy industry-ready front-end applications…
issues.Gap / Issue: Outcomes cover only the Front-end half of the program. Description scope adds SQL/RDBMS, Core/OOP Java, Data Structures and Algorithms, and Java Streams & Lambdas — none are reflected in the outcomes.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Add three outcome bullets:
• Design and query relational databases using SQL, ER modelling and normalization techniques to store, retrieve and manipulate data effectively.
• Apply data structures and algorithms (arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, hashing, searching, sorting and recursion) to solve programming problems efficiently.
• Write modern Java code using Generics, Collections, Lambdas, Functional Interfaces and the Stream API.

issues.Content: Page outcomes: 6) Develop and deploy industry-ready front-end applications aligned with modern web development standards and best practices.
issues.Gap / Issue: We dont deploy front-end applications
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'Develop and deliver industry-ready front-end applications, validated by comprehensive test cases, in alignment with modern web development standards and best practices'

issues.Content: Master Java Programming, OOP, and SQL to Build Scalable Applications with NIIT's Java Development Certificate Program.
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'Build entry-level Java developer skills across SQL databases, Object-Oriented Programming, Data Structures and Algorithms, and Streams & Lambdas — designed to make you job-ready for the Java Developer role.'

issues.Content: Outcome Driven; Practitioner Designed (from product description) — not present on page
issues.Gap / Issue: The product description's program sub-headline 'Outcome Driven; Practitioner Designed' is not surfaced anywhere on the page. This is a strong NIIT-wide differentiator that the description leads with.
issues.Fix Suggested: Add: 'Outcome Driven; Practitioner Designed' as a sub-headline below the program name.

issues.Content: The Java Development Certificate Program by NIIT helps learners build a strong foundation in Java programming and application development. The program covers core concepts such as Java fundamentals, object-oriented programming (OOP), exception handling, and database interaction using SQL. Through guided exercises and practical learning, participants develop coding and problem-solving skills while gaining hands-on experience in building basic Java applications.
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'This program develops the core competencies necessary for the Java Developer role at the entry level. Learners build the knowledge and skills required to work effectively with MySQL databases, Java object-oriented programming, Data Structures and Algorithms, and Lambdas and Streams. The program also emphasizes professional best practices and the right mindset for success. Through real-world challenges and hands-on projects, learners gain practical experience that prepares them to smoothly transition into the Java programming role.'

issues.Gap / Issue: Count is less
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with 15+

issues.Content: 10+ Practical Exercises
200+ LEARNING HOURS
issues.Fix Suggested: Update to: '212 Hours' (or round to '210+ Learning Hours').

issues.Content: Managing and Querying Database (RDBMS & SQL)
issues.Fix Suggested: Rename to: 'Managing and Querying Database'

issues.Content: Weeks 1–3 — 48 hours
issues.Gap / Issue: Course 1 has 10 learning sprints + 5 Course-End-Project sprints = 15 sprints @ 4 hrs each = 60 hours over 4 weeks. Page understates both hours and weeks.
issues.Fix Suggested: Update to: 'Weeks 1–4 — 60 Hours'

issues.Content: • Design and manage relational databases using SQL — Learn database design using ER diagrams and normalization techniques. Model and manage structured data using MySQL and retrieve information through SQL queries for effective data handling.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with:

• SQL Querying & Data Operations
Build strong hands-on skills to retrieve, analyze, and manipulate data using SQL, enabling you to extract meaningful insights and work effectively with real-world datasets.

• Database Design, Integrity & Advanced Capabilities
Design structured databases using ER Diagrams and define robust schemas while ensuring data consistency, and extend functionality through automation and support for modern data formats.

issues.Content: Module Activity
issues.Fix Suggested: Change label from 'Module Activity' to 'Activity'

issues.Content: Hands-on Project to Choose: Fitness Tracker Database, Movie Streaming Platform Database, Online Bookstore Database

issues.Fix Suggested: Hands-on Project to Choose:Hospital Patient Management System, Movie Streaming Platform Database, Recipe Manager Database, Student Attendance & Performance Tracker

issues.Content: Weeks 4–7 — 64 hours
issues.Gap / Issue: Course 2 has 10 learning sprints + 5 Course-End-Project sprints = 15 sprints @ 4 hrs each = 60 hours. Page overstates hours by 4 (one extra sprint), and the week range starts a week early — using continuous sprint numbering, sprints 16–30 → Weeks 4–8 (boundary week shared with Course 1).
issues.Fix Suggested: Update to: 'Weeks 4–8 — 60 Hours'

issues.Content: • Develop programming logic using Core Java and object-oriented concepts — Learn Java programming fundamentals, program structure, and problem-solving techniques. Understand how real-world entities are represented in software and implement object-oriented programs using Java.
issues.Fix Suggested:
1.Core Java Foundations
Build programs using Core Java with decision making, loops, arrays, strings, recursion, and basic searching and sorting to solve real problems.

2.Object-Oriented Programming
Design applications using object-oriented concepts and exception handling to create structured, real-world solutions.

issues.Content: Module Activity
issues.Fix Suggested: Change label from 'Module Activity' to 'Activity'

issues.Content: Hands-on Project to Choose: GreenPay – Smart Waste Collection & Reward System, DonateConnect – Community Donation and Distribution, EcoRide – Ride Sharing System, FarmConnect – Farmer-to-Market Supply Chain
issues.Gap / Issue: Order incorrect and missing project titles
issues.Fix Suggested: Hands-on Project to Choose: DonateConnect – A Community Donation and Distribution System, EventEase – Simple Event & Venue Management System, GreenPay – A Smart Waste Collection & Reward System, PetPal – Adoption Management for Pet Shelters, SafeNest – A Women Safety Alert and Response System, SkillMatch – A Simple Freelance Task Board

issues.Content: Weeks 8–10 — 48 hours
issues.Gap / Issue: Course 3 has 9 learning sprints (including 'DSA Project' sprint) + 5 Course-End-Project sprints = 14 sprints @ 4 hrs = 56 hours. Page understates hours and is one week short of expected (continuous sprint numbering puts C3 at sprints 31–43, i.e., Weeks 8–11).
issues.Fix Suggested: Course Name should be Data Structures and Algorithms Essentials and duration should be 52 hours

issues.Content: • Build strong problem-solving skills using data structures and algorithms — Understand key data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, and trees. Implement sorting, searching, and recursion techniques to build efficient and optimized programs.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with :
• Data Structures Implementation
Implement core data structures such as arrays, stacks, queues, linked lists, trees, and hash tables along with searching and sorting algorithms.

• Algorithm Analysis and Optimization
Analyse time and space complexity and apply recursion and suitable data structures to solve performance-critical problems.

issues.Content: Module Activity
issues.Fix Suggested: Change label from 'Module Activity' to 'Activity'

issues.Content: Hand-on Project to Choose: BuzzBuildSystem – A Social Media Hype Manager, Document Tagging & Retrieval System, Online Polling System with Real-Time Analytics, Security Alert Monitor with Real-Time Tracking

issues.Fix Suggested: Hands-on Project to Choose: BuzzBuildSystem – A Social Media Hype Manager, Document Tagging & Retrieval System, Local Services Search System (Just Dial Simulation), Security Alert Monitor with Real-Time Tracking

issues.Content: Advanced Java – Streams, Lambdas & Collections
issues.Fix Suggested: Rename to: 'Apply Advanced Java Concepts – Streams and Lambdas'

issues.Content: Weeks 11–13 — 48 hours
issues.Gap / Issue: Course 4 has 5 learning sprints + 5 Course-End-Project sprints = 10 sprints @ 4 hrs = 40 hours. Page overstates hours by 8 and shows the wrong week range — continuous sprint numbering puts C4 at sprints 44–53 → Weeks 11–14.
issues.Fix Suggested: Update to: 'Weeks 11–14 — 40 Hours'

issues.Content: • Write efficient and modern Java code using advanced programming techniques — Learn Java Collections, unit testing, and object comparison techniques. Implement Lambdas, functional interfaces, and Stream APIs to optimize code and improve performance.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace with

• Unit Testing and Collections
Write unit tests, enforce type safety with generics, and organize data using Java Collections with sorting.

• Functional Programming and Data Processing
Apply lambdas and functional interfaces, and process data using Streams with null-safe handling.

issues.Fix Suggested: Change label from 'Module Activity' to 'Activity'

issues.Fix Suggested: Hands-on Project to Choose: DonateConnect – A Community Donation and Distribution System, FarmConnect – A Farmer-to-Market Supply Chain, GreenPay – A Smart Waste Collection & Reward System, PetPal – Adoption Management for Pet Shelters, SafeNest – A Women Safety Alert and Response System, SkillMatch – A Simple Freelance Task Board

issues.Content: Comprehensive curriculum to build in-demand tech skills and real-world expertise. … 'master practical, in-demand skills' (Tools section intro).

issues.Content: Listed on page: Java, SQL.
issues.Gap / Issue: The list omits tools and technologies surfaced in the product description: MySQL (database engine), Maven (build tool), JUnit (testing), GenAI Tools (referenced in subqueries sprint).
issues.Fix Suggested: Add: 'MySQL, Maven, JUnit, GenAI Tools like ChatGPT, Gemini'

issues.Content: Capstone Project — Develop a functional application using Java that demonstrates core programming concepts and problem-solving skills. The project should incorporate object-oriented programming principles, data handling, and basic database integration to address a real-world scenario.
issues.Gap / Issue: Program has no Capstone Project course in the product description — the description shows 4 courses, each ending with its own Course-End Project. The term 'Capstone' should not appear on the page.
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace 'Capstone Project' with 'Project'.

issues.Content: Not present on page
issues.Gap / Issue: Each of the four courses has its own Course-End Project in the description's sprint structure (Module Activity items hint at these). The page collapses all four into a single generic 'Capstone Project' card, hiding the per-course portfolio depth.
issues.Fix Suggested:
Add six project cards:
1. Fitness Tracker Database - Design a fitness tracker database managing workouts, health data, subscriptions, and personalized progress tracking insights.
2. Online Bookstore Database — Create a bookstore database to manage books, orders, payments, inventory, and customer engagement insights.
3. EcoRide – Create a Java ride-sharing simulator with role-based menus for drivers and riders including booking and validation.
4. Online Polling System with Real-Time Analytics – Build a real-time polling system with vote processing, duplicate prevention, instant analytics, and rollback capabilities.
5. EventEase – Develop a Java event management app with venue scheduling, attendee registration, and edge-case handling for bookings.
6. FarmConnect – Design a farmer-to-buyer marketplace for listing, ordering, and tracking fresh produce with inventory management.

issues.Content: Boost Your Career Visibility — Showcasing a professional Capstone Project on your LinkedIn increases recruiter interest significantly. Build production-ready work that speaks for itself.
issues.Gap / Issue: Callout uses the term 'Capstone Project' which does not exist as a course in this program;
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'Boost Your Career Visibility — Showcasing your professional Course-End Projects on your LinkedIn increases recruiter interest significantly. Build production-ready work that speaks for itself.'

issues.Content: Page outcomes: 1) Design and manage relational databases using SQL... 2) Develop Java applications using core programming concepts... 3) Apply data structures and algorithms... 4) Write optimized and maintainable code using Java Collections, Lambdas, and Stream APIs. 5) Test and validate Java programs using unit testing and debugging techniques. 6) Build structured software solutions...
issues.Fix Suggested: Add a closing outcome bullet: 'Be job-ready for the entry-level Java Developer role by combining technical skills with professional best practices and the right engineering mindset.'
